# Claude Code Background Sessions & Agent View: Integration Reference for "endless"

## TL;DR
- Claude Code's background-agent system (branded **Agent View**, introduced in v2.1.139 on May 11, 2026, research preview) gives `endless` a clean integration surface: launch with `claude --bg "<prompt>"` (returns a short ID on stdout), discover/poll with `claude agents --json`, attach/promote to a dedicated terminal with `claude attach <id>`, and read on-disk state under `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/state.json` plus the daemon roster at `~/.claude/daemon/roster.json`.
- Viewing inside an active session (Space=peek, Enter=attach) does **not** terminate background status — sessions are hosted by a per-user supervisor (daemon) that runs them detached from any terminal; "attach" simply opens the running session in your current pane, and detaching (`←`, `Ctrl+Z`, `/exit`) leaves it running. To put a background agent in its own dedicated tmux window, run `claude attach <id>` in that window.
- The biggest integration hazards are: (1) quota scales linearly with N parallel agents — per the official docs, "running ten agents in parallel uses quota roughly ten times as fast as running one" — plus undocumented server-side burst/concurrency caps and a "thread limit"; (2) background sessions are **local only** and die on machine shutdown (recover with `claude respawn --all`); and (3) as of v2.1.139–2.1.143 the `template:"bg"` flag and `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` are set on *every* session including interactive ones (bug #59848), so they cannot reliably distinguish a true background agent.

## Key Findings

**1. Launch.** Three documented launch paths: `claude --bg "<prompt>"` (shell, scriptable), `/bg` or `/background` (from inside an interactive session), and typing a prompt into the `claude agents` dispatch input. `claude --bg` prints a short ID (~8 hex chars, git-hash-like) plus management commands to stdout. `--name` and `--agent` are supported.

**2. View vs. promote.** Inside a session, `←` (left arrow on empty prompt) or `/bg` backgrounds the current session and opens Agent View; Space peeks, Enter/→ attaches. Attaching is a *view that keeps the agent running*, not a promotion that kills the daemon copy. Detaching never stops it.

**3. Dedicated terminal.** `claude attach <id>` opens the running background session in the current terminal/tmux pane. The supervisor keeps hosting it; attach just connects a terminal to the already-running process.

**4. State model & discovery.** States: Working / Needs input / Idle / Completed / Failed / Stopped. Programmatic discovery: `claude agents --json`, files under `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/`, `~/.claude/daemon/roster.json`, and `claude daemon status`.

**5. SessionStart hook.** Fires for background agents; `/bg` resumes a saved conversation so it maps to `source:"resume"`, while fresh `claude --bg` is `source:"startup"`. No dedicated "background" source value or hook payload field.

**6. Daemon lifecycle.** A per-user supervisor process hosts sessions; survives terminal/shell close and auto-updates; sessions survive sleep (v2.1.142+) but die on shutdown.

**7. Concurrency & quota.** Linear quota burn (10×); soft "thread limit" cap and server-side burst limiter exist.

**8. Version & stability.** Introduced v2.1.139 (May 11, 2026); research preview; multiple open issues.

## Details

### 1. Launch mechanics

**Invocation.** Official docs document all three entry points. From the shell (the scriptable path for `endless`):

```
claude --bg "investigate the flaky SettingsChangeDetector test"
claude --agent code-reviewer --bg "address review comments on PR 1234"
claude --bg --name "flaky-test-fix" "investigate the flaky SettingsChangeDetector test"
```

`/bg` (alias of `/background`) backgrounds the current interactive session; you can append a final instruction (`/bg run the test suite and fix any failures`). Typing into the `claude agents` dispatch input launches a new background session per prompt.

**ID return form.** After `claude --bg`, Claude prints the session's short ID and a management command block to **stdout**. Documented example:

```
backgrounded · 7c5dcf5d · flaky-test-fix
  claude agents             list sessions
  claude attach 7c5dcf5d    open in this terminal
  claude logs 7c5dcf5d      show recent output
  claude stop 7c5dcf5d      stop this session
```

The short ID is also the directory name under `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/`. This is a human-formatted text block, not JSON — for machine consumption, `endless` should prefer `claude agents --json` (see §4) rather than parsing this. Note: there is a long-standing limitation that a running session cannot easily read its *own* session ID from inside (issues #25642, #44607); the `--bg` stdout ID is the reliable capture point at launch time.

**Config-flag inheritance at launch.** When backgrounding an existing session, these flags carry through to the backgrounded process: `--mcp-config`, `--strict-mcp-config`, `--settings`, `--add-dir`, `--plugin-dir`, `--fallback-model`, `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`, plus any `/add-dir` directories. Permission mode/model/effort persist across supervisor restarts (stored in the session's `respawnFlags`). Dispatching from the Agent View input or `claude --bg` uses the directory's `defaultMode`; `bypassPermissions`/`auto` are refused until accepted once interactively in that directory.

**Stdout/stderr semantics during launch.** `claude --bg` emits the backgrounded confirmation block to stdout and returns control to the shell immediately (it does not stream the agent's ongoing output — that goes to the supervisor-hosted session, retrievable later via `claude logs <id>`). A `--bg --exec '<cmd>'` variant runs a PTY-backed shell job instead of a model session; its captured output stays in memory (not on disk) and the row auto-cleans ~5 minutes after the command exits.

**Worktree isolation.** Every background session starts in your working directory but, before editing files, moves itself into an isolated git worktree under `.claude/worktrees/`. Disable via `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` (v2.1.143+). Outside a git repo there is no isolation. `endless` must account for the fact that a dispatched agent's edits land in a worktree, not the main checkout, until merged/pushed.

### 2. Resume / view from inside an active session

`claude agents` (or `←` from any session) opens Agent View. Select a row, press **Space** to peek (last output or pending question, reply inline), or **Enter/→** to **attach** — Agent View is replaced by the full interactive session. Official docs: "Each background session is a full Claude Code conversation that keeps running without a terminal attached." Attaching does **not** terminate background status; it connects a terminal to the running process, and Claude posts a short recap of what happened while you were away. Detaching via `←`, `Ctrl+Z`, `/exit`, or double `Ctrl+C`/`Ctrl+D` all leave the session running; only `/stop` (or `Ctrl+X` / `claude stop`) ends it.

**Concurrent viewing.** Multiple terminals can peek/inspect via `claude logs <id>` and `claude agents --json` simultaneously (read paths). The docs do not explicitly bless two *interactive attaches* to the same session at once; the model is one supervisor-hosted process that a terminal connects to. There are reports of attach instability (e.g., "claude agents attach occasionally bouncing straight back to the session list on the first try after a background-service restart," fixed in a later release). For `endless`, treat read/peek as freely concurrent and interactive attach as effectively single-consumer.

### 3. Promote to a dedicated tmux pane/terminal

The supported invocation is **`claude attach <id>`** (run it inside the target tmux window/pane). There is **no** new foreground process that replaces the daemon copy: the existing supervisor-hosted process continues running, and `claude attach` simply connects your terminal to it. The old process does **not** terminate, and detaching leaves it running. Attached sessions always render in fullscreen mode (a background session has no scrollback to append to), which affects how tmux copy-mode and native scroll behave — only the current viewport is visible to tmux.

Practical `endless` pattern: to give a chosen agent its own dedicated tmux window, `tmux new-window` (or `send-keys`) running `claude attach <id>`. `Alt+1`..`Alt+9` inside Agent View attach to sessions 1–9 in the focused directory.

### 4. State model and discovery

**State names (official).** The per-row state icon encodes one of:

| State | Icon | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Working | Animated | Actively running tools / generating |
| Needs input | Yellow | Waiting on a question or permission decision |
| Idle | Dimmed | Nothing to do; ready for next prompt |
| Completed | Green | Finished successfully |
| Failed | Red | Ended with an error |
| Stopped | Grey | Stopped via `Ctrl+X` or `claude stop` |

Separately, icon **shape** encodes process liveness: `✻`/animated `✽` = process alive; `∙` = process exited but resumable; `✢` = a `/loop` session sleeping between iterations. Note Agent View's *group* headers (Ready for review, Needs input, Working, Completed) don't map 1:1 to states — "Ready for review" means an open PR, and "Completed" collects finished+failed+stopped.

**Programmatic discovery (the core of `endless` integration):**

- **`claude agents --json`** — prints live sessions as a JSON array and exits. Each entry has `pid`, `cwd`, `kind`, and `startedAt`, plus `sessionId`, `name`, and `status` when set. When `status` is `waiting`, a `waitingFor` field says what it's blocked on (e.g., `permission prompt` or `input needed`). v2.1.145 added `--json`; a later release fixed it omitting blocked/just-dispatched sessions and **added `--all` (to include completed sessions) plus new `id` and `state` fields**. Combine with `--cwd <path>` to filter by project.
- **On-disk state files** (under `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` or `~/.claude`):
  - `~/.claude/daemon/roster.json` — list of running background sessions (used to reconnect after restart).
  - `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/state.json` — per-session state. Community-observed fields (issue #59848, captured on v2.1.143) are, verbatim: `{ "state": "blocked", "tempo": "active", … "template": "bg", "respawnFlags": ["--effort","high","--permission-mode","auto"], "name": "push-code-changes", "nameSource": "auto", … "cliVersion": "2.1.143", "cwd": "/Users/smabe/projects/HealthData" }` (ellipses denote undisclosed fields). The schema is **not officially documented** — treat as illustrative and version-fragile.
  - `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/tmp/` — per-session scratch dir; `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp` writes don't prompt for permission.
  - `~/.claude/daemon.log` — supervisor log.
- **`claude daemon status`** — reports whether the supervisor is reachable, its PID and version, the socket directory, and live session count. (Undocumented in the CLI reference per issue #58869, but functional; `/doctor` runs the same check.)

Each row's one-line summary is itself generated by "a Haiku-class model"; per the official docs, "While a session is actively working, the summary refreshes at most once every 15 seconds, plus once when each turn ends" — so the human-readable status text lags real-time by up to ~15s, another reason to read structured `state`/`status` fields rather than scraping summaries.

**Recommendation for `endless`:** poll `claude agents --json --all` as the primary, stable, supported discovery API; fall back to reading `roster.json` / `state.json` only for fields the JSON doesn't expose, and guard against schema drift.

### 5. SessionStart hook behavior in background sessions

**Does it fire?** Yes. SessionStart "runs on every session." The `source` field enumerates only `startup` / `resume` / `clear` / `compact`. Because backgrounding "starts a fresh process that resumes from the saved conversation," a `/bg` or `←` background maps to **`source:"resume"`** (strong inference from the documented resume mechanism + the documented "resume → `source:'resume'`" rule; *not* an explicit doc statement for `/bg`). A fresh `claude --bg "<prompt>"` with no prior conversation is a new session → **`source:"startup"`**. There is **no** dedicated `"background"`/`"bg"` source value.

**Any hook payload field that says "this is a background agent"? No.** The documented SessionStart stdin payload is `session_id`, `transcript_path`, `cwd`, `hook_event_name`, `source`, `model`, and optionally `agent_type` and `session_title`. There is no `template`, `sessionKind`, or `is_background` field in the hook JSON. `agent_id`/`agent_type` identify *subagents*, not daemon-hosted background sessions.

**Indirect detection — and why it's unreliable.** A hook process inherits the parent environment, so it *could* read `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` or `cat $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/state.json` to check `template`. But issue #59848 documents that, post-v2.1.139, **every** session — including a plain interactive `claude` — gets `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR`, a daemon-managed state file, and `template:"bg"`. So as of v2.1.139–2.1.143, neither `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` presence nor `template:"bg"` reliably distinguishes a real background agent. The proposed fix (reserve `template:"bg"` for genuinely spawned sessions, set `template:"foreground"` otherwise) is *proposed, not shipped* in those versions. `endless` should therefore identify background agents via `claude agents --json` membership rather than via hook-side environment sniffing.

**Environment inheritance for hook context.** `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` is set to `~/.claude/jobs/<id>` for each background session (documented). `CLAUDECODE=1` is set in subprocesses Claude Code spawns (Bash/PowerShell tools, tmux sessions, hook commands, statusline, stdio MCP servers). `CLAUDE_ENV_FILE` is available in SessionStart/Setup/CwdChanged/FileChanged hooks to persist env vars for the session. The per-user supervisor persists independently with its own long-lived environment rather than cleanly inheriting each spawning shell — confirmed by environment-parity bugs where the bg boot path drops user agents (#58729) and Claude.ai connectors (#58353) that interactive sessions load. (`CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` is a known internal launch-tag variable but was not confirmed in the env-vars docs or the bg env dumps reviewed; treat its bg value as unverified.) Caution: a SessionStart hook that spawns a backgrounded child holding stdin/stdout can deadlock the session (issue #43123, after v2.1.87 tightened subprocess comms) — relevant because hook execution context changed in the same v2.1.139 release (command hooks now run without terminal access).

**Known SessionStart reliability bugs:** historically (issue #10373) SessionStart hook stdout was not injected into context for brand-new interactive sessions in some builds (worked for `/clear`, `/compact`, URL-resume). On the VSCode extension, `/clear` reported `source:"startup"` instead of `"clear"` (#49937). `endless` should not assume the `source` value is perfectly reliable across surfaces.

### 6. Daemon lifecycle

A **per-user supervisor process** hosts every background session, separate from your terminal and Agent View. It starts automatically the first time you background a session or open Agent View, and exits on its own when no sessions remain and no terminal is connected. It is keyed to `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (setting that runs a separate supervisor instance with its own sessions). The supervisor persists independently with its own long-lived environment rather than cleanly inheriting each spawning shell — confirmed by the environment-parity bugs noted above.

**Effects of machine events:**
- **Closing the spawning terminal / shell:** no effect — sessions keep running (detached, supervisor-hosted).
- **Machine sleep:** preserved. Per the official docs, "Sessions are also preserved when your machine sleeps. Their processes resume on wake and the supervisor reconnects to them instead of treating the time gap as idle." v2.1.142+ the daemon detects clock jumps to do this. (The agent-view doc page was reported temporarily outdated on this point — issue #59263.)
- **Machine shutdown / restart:** Per the official docs, "Shutting down still stops running sessions." Running sessions show as **Failed** on next open. Recovery: attach/peek/reply to restart from where it left off, or `claude respawn --all` to restart all at once.
- **Idle reaping:** Per the official docs and field reports, "After a session has been idle and unattached for about an hour, the supervisor stops the underlying process to free resources — peek or attach and Claude restarts it from where it left off." State persists on disk. Pinned sessions (`Ctrl+T`) are exempt and restart in place across updates.
- **Auto-update:** supervisor watches the binary on disk (local file watch, not network) and restarts into the new version; detached sessions survive; `claude respawn <id>` / `--all` moves sessions onto a new binary.

**Recovery semantics:** respawn/restart **resumes** the saved conversation/state (not a clean start). Transcript lives at `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<full-session-id>.jsonl` and remains resumable via `claude --resume <full-id>` even after a job is deleted from Agent View (#58725).

**Recovery command for `endless`:** if attach/peek/`claude logs` reports "background service did not respond," the supervisor stalled — `claude daemon stop --any --keep-workers` restarts the supervisor while keeping sessions alive.

### 7. Concurrency and quota

**Quota.** Official limitation, verbatim from the Agent View docs: "Rate limits apply: background sessions consume your subscription usage the same as interactive sessions, so running ten agents in parallel uses quota roughly ten times as fast as running one." There is **no separate agent billing**. Each row summary is also a small Haiku-class request (refreshes at most every 15s plus once per turn-end), billed to your quota.

**Soft caps / throttling (community-observed):**
- A **"thread limit"** soft ceiling on concurrent session dispatch (reported: "One QA/accessibility reviewer did not spawn because the thread limit is reached"). This is a concurrency cap distinct from the rate-limit quota.
- A **server-side burst/concurrency limiter** that fires when many sessions bootstrap simultaneously (issue #53922: first 3–4 of ~10 rapid-fire sessions succeed, the rest get "Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited" until retried with delay).
- **Per-model throughput (RPM/TPM)** limits bite hardest on Opus; multiple parallel Opus sessions hit throughput caps well before the usage cap.
- Supervisor switching/deadlock pauses (30–60s) reported when rapidly switching between freshly dispatched sessions.
- There is **no** user-facing `maxParallelAgents` setting (requested in #15487); Anthropic does not publish a hard cap on simultaneous sessions.

**Practical ceilings (community guidance).** The FindSkill.ai "Week 1 Failure Modes" field report concludes that "Three to five parallel sessions is the sweet spot for most users," with ~2 the realistic ceiling on Pro before hitting thread limits, switching deadlocks, and supervisor pauses. `endless` should implement its own dispatch throttling (stagger launches, exponential backoff with jitter on the "temporarily limiting requests" error) rather than relying on Claude Code to queue.

### 8. Version availability and stability

**Introduction.** Agent View shipped in **Claude Code v2.1.139**, announced by Anthropic on **May 11, 2026** (the GitHub CHANGELOG entry is dated May 11–12). `claude --bg`, `/bg` (alias `/background`), and `claude agents` all arrived with this release; `/goal` shipped in the same version. Background *agents* (the underlying send-to-background + `/tasks` capability) predate Agent View (community references to v2.0.60). Availability: research preview on **Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise, and Claude API** plans (note: one report — issue #58570 — found Agent View *not* enabled for some Claude API users on v2.1.140 despite the docs; verify per-account). Free plan: interactive only, no background agents.

**Version progression of relevant capabilities:**
- v2.1.139 — Agent View + `claude agents` + `/bg` + `--bg` (research preview).
- v2.1.141 — `claude agents --cwd <path>` scoping; `claude daemon status` referenced.
- v2.1.142 — dispatch flags (`--permission-mode`, `--model`, `--effort`, `--dangerously-skip-permissions`); macOS sleep/wake reconnect fix.
- v2.1.143 — `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`; `worktree.bgIsolation` setting.
- v2.1.144 — `/resume` support for background sessions (marked with `bg`).
- v2.1.145 — `claude agents --json`; awaiting-input tab counts.
- v2.1.147 — pinned background sessions stay alive when idle, restart in place for updates.
- v2.1.157 — `--agent` honored for dispatched sessions.
- A later release — `claude agents --json` `--all`/`id`/`state` fields fix.

**Current support.** As of this report (June 12, 2026), the feature is shipping. Per the LaoZhang AI blog, "As of May 19, 2026, the npm latest tag for @anthropic-ai/claude-code was 2.1.143"; subsequently v2.1.162 released June 3, with v2.1.168/2.1.169 referenced in later changelogs. Use `claude --version` and confirm `claude agents` opens the dashboard rather than listing subagents.

**Known regressions / open issues (stability):**
- **#59848** — interactive sessions misclassified as background (`template:"bg"`, `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` on foreground sessions), triggering bg-only worktree-isolation guards on interactive edits. *Most important for `endless`'s detection logic.*
- **#58729** — background sessions don't load user agents from `~/.claude/agents/` into the Agent tool registry.
- **#58570** — Agent View not enabled for some Claude API users on v2.1.140.
- **#58725** — `/resume` picker omits background sessions.
- **#59263** — agent-view sleep/wake docs outdated for v2.1.142.
- **#58869** — `claude daemon status` undocumented.
- **#43123** — SessionStart hook with a backgrounded child process deadlocks the session.
- **#65971** — conversational mention of "workflow" can trigger dynamic workflows and leave a persistent daemon that hijacks `claude` into Agent View.
- Various TUI/IDE attach and keyboard-focus bugs (WSL frame corruption; VSCode agents-manager focus loss on 2.1.168, #66015).
- Documented fixes show active churn: EAUTH on attach after daemon auto-update, busy-spinner lag, stale frames, truncated status text, image-paste no-ops.

Because the feature is explicitly **research preview**, Anthropic warns the interface and keyboard shortcuts may change. `endless` should pin to the stable surfaces (CLI subcommands and `--json`) and avoid depending on TUI keybindings or undocumented `state.json` fields.

## Recommendations

**Stage 1 — Minimal viable integration (depend only on documented, stable surfaces).**
1. **Dispatch** child tasks with `claude --bg --name "<endless-task-id>" "<prompt>"` (optionally `--agent`), and **capture the short ID from stdout** at launch (parse the `backgrounded · <id> · <name>` line). Setting `--name` to your own task ID makes the row identifiable in `--json` output.
2. **Discover/poll** with `claude agents --json --all` (filter by `--cwd` when you want one project). Map its `state`/`status`/`waitingFor` fields to your task board. Poll on an interval (e.g., 5–15s) rather than tailing files; remember row summaries lag up to ~15s, so key your logic on structured state, not summary text.
3. **Promote to a dedicated tmux window** by opening a new window/pane running `claude attach <id>`. Don't expect this to change the agent's lifecycle — the supervisor keeps hosting it, and detaching leaves it running.
4. **Stop/clean up** with `claude stop <id>` and `claude rm <id>` (remember `claude rm` keeps worktrees with uncommitted changes and prints their path; deleting in the TUI via `Ctrl+X Ctrl+X` discards the worktree).

**Stage 2 — Robustness.**
5. **Throttle your own dispatch**: cap concurrent launches (start at ≤3, configurable; 3–5 is the community sweet spot), stagger them, and implement exponential backoff with jitter on "Server is temporarily limiting requests" and 429/529. Do not assume Claude Code queues for you.
6. **Pin** long-running monitor agents (`Ctrl+T` semantics) or keep them active; otherwise expect ~1h idle reaping and a cold-start delay on next attach.
7. **Handle shutdown/sleep**: on host restart, detect Failed sessions and offer `claude respawn --all`; treat sleep as recoverable on v2.1.142+.
8. **Daemon health**: surface `claude daemon status`; on "background service did not respond," run `claude daemon stop --any --keep-workers`.

**Stage 3 — Advanced / fragile (guard behind capability checks).**
9. Read `~/.claude/jobs/<id>/state.json` and `roster.json` only for fields `--json` doesn't expose, and version-guard against schema drift.
10. For hook-based eventing, register a SessionStart hook but **do not** rely on it to distinguish background vs. foreground (the `template`/`$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR` signals are unreliable through v2.1.143). Identify background agents via `claude agents --json` membership instead.

**Thresholds that change the plan:**
- If Anthropic ships the #59848 fix (reliable `template:"foreground"` vs `"bg"`), hook-side background detection becomes viable — revisit Stage 3.10.
- If a documented `maxParallelAgents`/queueing setting ships (#15487), you can relax your own throttling (Stage 2.5).
- If Agent View exits research preview, the `--json` schema and TUI keybindings stabilize enough to depend on more directly.
- If a cloud/remote supervisor (cross-machine roster sync) ships, revisit the "local only / dies on shutdown" assumption.

## Caveats
- **Research preview**: interface, shortcuts, state names, and on-disk schema may change without notice; disableable via the `disableAgentView` setting or `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AGENT_VIEW` environment variable (administrators can enforce via managed settings).
- **Inference flags**: the exact SessionStart `source` value for `/bg` (resume) vs `claude --bg` (startup) is a strong inference from documented mechanisms, not an explicit doc statement.
- **Community vs official**: state-icon names, `claude agents --json` field names, the supervisor model, `CLAUDE_JOB_DIR`, the Haiku summary cadence, and the 10× quota multiplier are from **official docs**; the `state.json` field list, "thread limit," burst limiter, switching deadlock, and practical 3–5 parallel ceiling are **community/issue-reported** and version-fragile.
- **`claude daemon status` and the full `state.json` schema are undocumented**; do not treat as a stable API.
- **Local only**: background sessions are not cloud-synced; there is no cross-machine roster. For cross-machine work, Claude Code on the web is a separate product that runs sessions in Anthropic's cloud and survives machine sleep.
- **Worktree side effects**: dispatched agents edit in `.claude/worktrees/`, and deleting a session in Agent View deletes its worktree (including uncommitted changes) — `endless` must surface "merge/push before delete."
- **Account variance**: some Claude API accounts reportedly lacked Agent View even on a qualifying version (#58570); always verify with `claude agents` at runtime.

### Source URLs
- Official: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-view · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agents · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/whats-new · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog · https://code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/sessions · https://claude.com/blog/agent-view-in-claude-code
- GitHub (anthropics/claude-code): CHANGELOG https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md · Releases https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases · Issues #59848, #58729, #58570, #58725, #59263, #58869, #43123, #65971, #66015, #53922, #15487, #10373, #49937, #25642, #44607
- Community: https://www.dsebastien.net/claude-code-agent-view/ · https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/2384/claude-code-agent-view-cli-dashboard-sessions-2026 · https://findskill.ai/blog/claude-code-10-parallel-agents-week-1/ · https://www.cloudzero.com/blog/claude-code-agents/ · https://blog.laozhang.ai/en/posts/claude-code-agent-view · https://changelogs.directory/tools/claude-code/releases/2.1.139 · https://wmedia.es/en/tips/claude-code-agent-view-parallel-sessions · https://developertoolkit.ai/en/claude-code/advanced-techniques/agent-view/

payload.title: Migrate Justification-heading notes to typed task_content rows
payload.description: When E-1531 ships typed task_content, migrate existing notes-stored justifications written by E-1544 (## Justification heading in tasks.notes) to a task_content row of type=justification.

fields.text:
# E-1552 — Decide endless task spawn behavior when invoked on various task types

## 1. Context

`endless task spawn` today claims a task, creates its worktree, opens a tmux
window, and pastes a Python `string.Template`-rendered handoff. The flow is
**type-blind**: spawn reads only `id`, `title`, `status`, `project_id`. The
handoff is a single document assuming the spawned session implements code and
ends in `verify`.

E-1538 lands `task_types` FK seeding (`task`, `bug`, `research`, `epic`).
E-1537 adopts `epic` as a coordinator-only type that never implements code
directly. That breaks today's spawn assumption: spawning an epic with the
current handoff produces a session that tries to implement a container.

The wider problem is tmux-window proliferation. Today's spawn opens a fresh
window per task. For an epic dispatching N children, that's 1 + N windows the
user must task-switch between. The May 2026 release of Claude Code's Agent
View (`claude --bg`, `claude agents`, `claude attach`) gives us a load-bearing
alternative: child sessions run as supervisor-hosted bg agents with no tmux
window of their own, viewable in-place from the coordinator's pane, promotable
to a dedicated window only when focus is needed.

This task delivers a single coherent design covering:
- All 24 cells of {`task`, `bug`, `research`, `epic`} × children-state.
- Bg-agent dispatch as the v1 default for epic children.
- Type-aware handoff templates rendered via Go `text/template`.
- DB and CLI changes needed to support coordinator + bg-agent operation.

Inputs:
- `docs/research-2026-06-10-claude-multi-agent-usage.md` (Model 1/2 framing).
- `docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md` (Agent View mechanics,
  `claude --bg`, `claude attach`, `worktree.bgIsolation`, recovery).
- E-1537 §6.5 — coordinator session semantics.
- E-1538 — `task_types` FK seeding (must land first).

## 2. The matrix

Design space: {`task`, `bug`, `research`, `epic`} × {zero, all `needs_plan`,
all `ready`, all `in_progress`, all terminal, mixed children}.

**Key reduction.** For types `task`, `bug`, `research`, the spawned session
works on **the parent task itself** — children are follow-ups (verification
matrices, drive-by discoveries, sub-research). Children-state doesn't change
what spawn does at the CLI level; it surfaces in the handoff as context.

For type=`epic`, the spawned session is a coordinator (E-1537 §6.5); children
ARE the work. Children-state varies what the coordinator **does inside its
session**, not what spawn does at the CLI level.

Conclusion: **spawn's CLI flow stays uniform across all 24 cells; the handoff
template branches.** Same pattern endless already uses for type-conditional
behavior (verb gates, completable-status gates).

## 3. Per-type spawn behavior

| Type | Spawn behavior | Handoff variant | End-state |
|---|---|---|---|
| `task` | Today's foreground tmux + claim | `handoff/task.md.tmpl` | `verify` → confirmed |
| `bug` | Same as task; reproduce-first framing | `handoff/bug.md.tmpl` | `verify` → confirmed |
| `research` | Same as task; findings-as-deliverable | `handoff/research.md.tmpl` | `completed` (verb gate per ED-1502) |
| `epic` | Foreground tmux coordinator + children-state summary | `handoff/epic.md.tmpl` | `completed` via auto-derivation (E-1537 §4) |

The coordinator session itself runs foreground tmux — interactive driver,
needs the user. Bg dispatch applies to its CHILDREN, not the coordinator
itself.

For all types, handoffs include the current children count and recommend the
spawned session read with `--children` flag. Zero renders as "(no children)";
no special case.

## 4. Bg-agent dispatch (the epic-child mechanism)

### 4.1 Carrier mechanism — `--name` binding

`endless task spawn --bg <id>` is the dispatch verb. It:

1. Validates the done-ish-status gate (today's set of statuses that require `--force` to claim over: `verify`, `confirmed`, `declined`, `obsolete`, `assumed`, `completed`).
2. Pre-claims: creates worktree + flips status to `in_progress` (today's
   `_perform_claim_work` with `target_session=None`).
3. Renders the type-appropriate handoff to a temp file.
4. Launches `claude --bg --name "E-<task_id>" "$(cat handoff.md)"` as a
   subprocess. Captures the short ID from stdout (the
   `backgrounded · <id> · <name>` line).
5. Writes the binding row directly: `sessions(task_id, claude_short_id,
   kind='background', active_epic_id, started_at)`.

No DB-token table, no env-var carrier, no hook-side lookup. Claude's `--name`
parameter IS the carrier; `claude agents --json` is the live discovery
surface. SessionStart hook still fires inside the bg agent (source=`startup`)
and may decorate the row with the full `session_id` from hook payload, but
isn't load-bearing.

E-1558 (filed) tracks the future addition of a DB-token mechanism for
non-Claude harnesses.

### 4.2 `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` — REQUIRED for v1

Per the Agent View docs, `claude --bg` creates a nested isolation worktree
under `.claude/worktrees/` before edits. That defeats endless's pre-claim
(the bg agent would edit Claude's worktree, not the tracker's). The
`worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` setting (v2.1.143+) disables it.

`just claude-settings-init` already manages worktree-local
`.claude/settings.json`. Extend it to set `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` so
bg agents stay in the launch cwd (the tracker worktree).

E-1559 (filed) tracks the long-term `WorktreeCreate` hook alternative. Open
issue density (~11 open issues, two regressions in June 2026, including
#68214's critical isolation-failure bug) means this stays maybe-phase until
Agent View exits research preview.

### 4.3 Throttling — soft warning

Quota scales linearly with parallel bg agents (10× for 10 agents).
Community sweet spot is 3–5 parallel; Pro plans hit thread limits at ~2.

`endless task spawn --bg <id>` issues a soft warning at dispatch when N bg
agents are already active for the project (default threshold 3,
configurable in `.endless/config.json` as `bg_throttle_warn`). Does NOT
block dispatch — coordinator decides whether to proceed. Warning text
surfaces the quota cost and thread-limit risk.

### 4.4 Children-state computation

Coordinator's epic handoff includes a state breakdown computed at
render-time:

```sql
SELECT status, count(*) FROM tasks WHERE parent_id = ? GROUP BY status
```

Rendered as e.g. "3 ready, 2 needs_plan, 1 in_progress, 4 terminal". Lets
the coordinator session pick its operational mode without re-querying.

### 4.5 Coordinator operational modes (in handoff)

The epic handoff guides the coordinator session into the right mode based
on the rendered state breakdown:

- **Zero children**: drive decomposition. Plan-mode + AskUserQuestion +
  parallel Explore sub-agents for context. File children via `endless task
  add --parent <epic-id>`.
- **All `needs_plan`**: Model 1 (planning orchestrator). Draft each child's
  plan inline; user reviews; attach via `endless task update <child> --text`.
- **All `ready`**: Model 2 (dispatcher). Run `endless task spawn --bg
  <child-id>` for each child (subject to throttling warning). Monitor via
  `claude agents --json --all` or `endless session list`.
- **All `in_progress`**: observe. No new dispatch. Surface child statuses.
- **All terminal**: epic should be `completed` via auto-derivation. Ask
  whether to reopen (add children) or accept as done.
- **Mixed**: surface breakdown; ask user which mode to start in. No
  auto-action.

## 5. Attach mechanisms

Two distinct verbs:

- **`endless task spawn --attach <id>`** — opens a NEW tmux window for an
  existing bg agent. Mechanism: `tmux new-window`; send-keys to run
  `claude attach <short-id>`. The supervisor keeps hosting the bg agent;
  attach just connects the new pane to it. Used when a child agent needs
  dedicated focus separate from the coordinator's pane.

- **`endless task attach <id>`** — exec into the bg agent IN THE CURRENT
  pane. Mechanism: `os.execvp("claude", ["attach", short_id])`. The
  endless Python process is replaced by claude in-place. Use case:
  sitting at a fresh shell, want to drop into a running bg agent without
  opening a new window.

Both detach paths (`Ctrl+Z`, `/exit`, `←`) leave the bg agent running.
Only `claude stop <id>` or `Ctrl+X` ends it.

## 6. Handoff templates

### 6.1 Format and renderer

Templates migrate from Python `string.Template` to **Go `text/template`**.
Renderer: a hidden `endless internal template render <name>` subcommand
that reads a JSON vars blob on stdin and writes the rendered output to
stdout. Python spawn shells out per render.

### 6.2 Location

`templates/` (top-level repo dir). Sub-organized:

- `templates/handoff/task.md.tmpl`
- `templates/handoff/bug.md.tmpl`
- `templates/handoff/research.md.tmpl`
- `templates/handoff/epic.md.tmpl`
- `templates/coordinator/explore-child-context.md.tmpl`
- `templates/coordinator/plan-child.md.tmpl`
- `templates/coordinator/dispatch-child-impl.md.tmpl`
- `templates/coordinator/verify-child.md.tmpl`

The existing `docs/templates/handoff.md` migrates to
`templates/handoff/task.md.tmpl` (with syntax conversion `$var` → `{{.Var}}`).

`templates/` is the home for now. Revisit on E-1486 (Python → Go port);
likely moves to a Go-embedded location at that point.

### 6.3 Coordinator templates

Endless ships a SET of templates the coordinator session uses for
sub-tasks (Explore context-gathering, child plan drafting, dispatch
handoff, verify-reviewer). Coordinator can override locally but defaults
to template. Keeps prompts evolvable and consistent across coordinators.

## 7. DB schema changes

Single new column:

- `sessions.active_epic_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) NULL`

Semantics:

| Session kind | `active_task_id` | `active_epic_id` |
|---|---|---|
| Non-epic foreground (today) | task being worked | NULL |
| Epic coordinator | initially = epic id; changes as user switches view | epic id (stable) |
| Bg agent dispatched from coordinator | child task id | parent epic id |

Bg agent rows live in `sessions` alongside coordinator rows. `tmux_pane`
is NULL for bg agents. Window-name rendering reads `active_epic_id +
active_task_id`:

- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND active_epic_id != active_task_id`
  → `[E-EEEE:E-CCCC]`
- `active_epic_id IS NOT NULL AND = active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE]`
- `active_epic_id IS NULL` → `[E-NNNN]` (today's behavior)

"Currently focused bg agent" is **derived**: a bg agent is focused iff
`bg.active_task_id = coordinator.active_task_id AND bg.active_epic_id =
coordinator.active_epic_id`. No stored `focused` flag.

`sessions.kind` discriminator added: `tmux` (today's default) or
`background`. Used to filter `endless session list` views and to NULL the
`tmux_pane` constraint for bg rows.

## 8. Session lifecycle and recovery

Bg agents survive: foreground Claude exit, terminal close, tmux pane
close, machine sleep (v2.1.142+).
Bg agents die on: machine shutdown, 1h idle (unpinned), `claude stop`.

Recovery surfaces (filed as follow-ups, NOT in scope for E-1552):

- **E-1560** (next): `endless session restore [<id>|--all]` wraps
  `claude respawn` for post-reboot recovery.
- **E-1561** (next): `endless tmux restore` reconnects to surviving bg
  agents and respawns a coordinator after tmux crash.

E-1552's design assumes these will exist. The CLI surface today should
not actively prevent them from being added.

## 9. Status gate behavior

The done-ish-status gate stays uniform across types: the statuses
`verify`, `confirmed`, `declined`, `obsolete`, `assumed`, `completed`
require `--force` to spawn. Same for all types and both fg/bg paths.

For epic in `completed` (auto-derived): `--force` is "reopen this
initiative" — the epic handoff frames it as reopen.

## 10. Resolution of original open questions

| Original question | Resolution |
|---|---|
| Resolution mechanism: auto / `--mode` / interactive? | Auto-detect children-state at render-time; coordinator chooses operational mode in-session. No CLI mode flag. |
| Does orchestrator claim the epic? | Yes. Coordinator pre-claims the epic via today's spawn flow. |
| Children remain claimable by own sessions? | Each child's claim happens at `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>` time (deterministic, Go/Python-side), not the coordinator's responsibility. |
| Worktree creation timing | Per-child worktree created by `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>` at dispatch time. Not pre-claimed all at once. |
| Agent View integration | `claude agents --json --all` is the canonical bg-agent state source. `endless session list` queries it for live state. |
| Explore sub-agent dispatch | Endless ships a SET of coordinator templates in `templates/coordinator/`. Coordinator selects per use-case; can override locally. |
| Relationship to E-1136 | Co-exist. E-1552 = interactive coordinator. E-1136 = unattended daemon. Different operational shapes. |

## 11. Implementation tasks (to file after this lands)

Each with `--cleans-up E-1552`. Filed only after Mike approves the plan.

1. **Template migration to Go `text/template`** — add
   `endless internal template render`; migrate existing handoff;
   establish `templates/` location.
2. **Per-type handoff variants** — split into 4 templates; plumb
   `task_types.slug` into the renderer; fallback to `task.md.tmpl`.
3. **Children-state summary injection** — query and pass to the epic
   handoff renderer.
4. **`endless task spawn --bg`** — capture stdout short ID, write binding
   row, set `--name "E-<task_id>"`.
5. **`worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` in `just claude-settings-init`**.
6. **`endless task spawn --attach`** and **`endless task attach`** — both
   wrap `claude attach <short-id>`.
7. **`sessions.active_epic_id` column** + window-name rendering update.
8. **`sessions.kind` discriminator** + `endless session list` bg awareness.
9. **Soft throttling warning** at dispatch when N bg agents already
   active for the project.
10. **Guide doc update** — `endless guide orchestration` documents the
    coordinator pattern, bg dispatch, attach verbs.

Coordinator template SET (`templates/coordinator/*.md.tmpl`) can ship
incrementally — file the first set as one task once the renderer and
spawn-bg path land.

## 12. Verification

1. **Type detection wiring** — spawn each of `task`, `bug`, `research`,
   `epic` test tasks; confirm the right handoff renders.
2. **Children-state summary correctness** — file an epic with mixed
   child statuses, spawn it, confirm rendered summary matches
   `endless task show <epic> --children`.
3. **Bg dispatch end-to-end** — coordinator runs `endless task spawn
   --bg <child>`; bg agent appears in `claude agents --json` with name
   `E-<child-id>`; binding row in `sessions` matches.
4. **`bgIsolation:"none"` verified** — bg agent's edits land in the
   endless tracker worktree, not under `.claude/worktrees/`.
5. **Attach paths** — `task spawn --attach <child>` opens new tmux
   window running `claude attach <id>`; `task attach <child>` execs
   into claude in current pane.
6. **Throttling warning** — N+1th bg dispatch emits warning, proceeds.
7. **Session restoration story** — after machine sleep+wake, bg agents
   resume; after stop+respawn, agents recoverable via `claude respawn`.
8. **Status gate unchanged** — spawn on done-ish-status non-epic tasks
   refuses without `--force`; with `--force`, demotes and dispatches.

## 13. Out of scope / deferred

- **WorktreeCreate hook integration** — E-1559 (maybe).
- **Non-Claude harness carrier mechanism** — E-1558 (maybe).
- **Session restoration commands** — E-1560, E-1561 (next).
- **`endless coordinator` daemon** — E-1136 stays as unattended-mode
  counterpart; not obsoleted by this work.
- **`endless epic` CLI surface** (alias group) — owned by E-1540.

# E-1444: Allow endless task add from plain terminal without a Claude session

## Context

The E-1401 attribution gate in `src/endless/event_bridge.py:emit_event()` (lines ~99-108) refuses any event whose `actor_kind` is in `_ATTRIBUTION_REQUIRED = {"cli", "hook"}` unless a session_id can be resolved. The gate exists for a real reason: every event SHOULD carry session attribution so per-session activity queries (E-1284, E-1322 session_tasks) stay meaningful and audit trails aren't silently truncated.

Today the gate fires before `--force` is consulted, with no escape hatch for legitimate non-Claude-pane callers (triage filings from a plain shell, scripts, cron, post-incident ledger entries). The error message lists three remediations — run inside a Claude pane, export `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID`, or `task bind` to a sibling — and `--force` is not among them, so callers don't discover any bypass even if one existed.

Reproducer (verified during E-1337 filing):
```sh
endless task add "probe" --type chore             # refused, no session
endless task add "probe" --type chore --force     # still refused; --force ignored
```

The bug is self-recursive: a session filing the ticket about this gate is itself blocked by the gate unless it has a live Claude pane.

## Approach (decided 2026-05-21 with Mike)

Add a new `--no-session` flag implemented **once** as a shared option, applied to every CLI verb whose `emit_event` call sets `actor_kind` to `cli` or `hook` and therefore goes through the attribution gate today. The flag downgrades the event's `actor.kind` from `cli` to `system` — `system` is already exempt from `_ATTRIBUTION_REQUIRED`, so the existing gate code needs no logic change beyond accepting the downgrade.

The audit of which verbs need the flag is part of the implementation, not a separate task. The principle: **the flag attaches to commands that need a session today**, not to all write commands. Read-only verbs and commands that already use `actor_kind="system"` are untouched.

### Shared-flag implementation (Click)

Click supports option sharing via:
1. **A reusable decorator** — define `no_session_option` once and apply with `@no_session_option` on each command function. Cleanest read at the call site.
2. **A custom `click.Group` subclass** that adds the option to every subcommand automatically — DRY but magical (every command, even ones that don't need it, gains a useless flag).
3. **Click's `add_command_option`/eager-option patterns** — less common, more boilerplate.

**Choice:** option 1 (reusable decorator). Each command opts in explicitly, so the audit is visible in code review. No silent inheritance of flags by commands that shouldn't have them.

```python
# src/endless/cli_helpers.py (or similar)
no_session_option = click.option(
    "--no-session",
    is_flag=True,
    help="File this command without a Claude session binding "
         "(actor.kind='system'). Use when running from a plain shell, "
         "cron, or script.",
)
```

Each command then:
```python
@worktree_cmd.command("...")
@no_session_option
def cmd(no_session, ...):
    ...
```

The command receives `no_session: bool` and passes it through to `emit_event` (which gains a new parameter or, simpler, the caller flips `actor_kind` to `"system"` before invocation).

### Considered alternatives (rejected)

- **Widen `--force`** to also bypass attribution. Rejected: `--force` already overloads two semantics in `task claim` (status-demotion bypass + no-binding-required); adding a third blurs intent. Shell history of `endless task add --force` reads as "I'm overriding something" but doesn't say what.
- **Auto-downgrade `actor.kind` to `system` when stdout is not a tty AND no session resolves.** Rejected: implicit attribution loss is brittle. An interactive user piping output (`endless task list | less`) becomes not-a-tty and silently files without attribution. Hides the attribution miss in cases where the user intended a session-bound filing but mis-configured their environment. The opt-in approach surfaces the intent.

## Error message update

`event_bridge.py:99-108` gains a fourth bullet naming the new flag:

```
Cannot determine the Endless session for this pane.

To fix, do one of:
  - Run this command from a Claude session pane.
  - Export ENDLESS_SESSION_ID="$(endless session id)".
  - Run `endless task bind <task-id>` from this pane to connect it to a
    sibling Claude session in the same tmux window.
  - Pass --no-session to file without a Claude session binding (sets
    actor.kind=system; suitable for cron, scripts, and plain-shell
    triage filings).
```

## Implementation sites

- `src/endless/event_bridge.py:24` — `_ATTRIBUTION_REQUIRED` constant (untouched; `system` is already exempt).
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py:99-108` — error string update.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py:27-159` — `emit_event` accepts the bypass either via a new parameter or via the caller pre-downgrading `actor_kind`. Caller-side downgrade is simpler — no new parameter.
- New file `src/endless/cli_helpers.py` (or extend an existing helpers module) — define `no_session_option` decorator.
- `src/endless/cli.py` — apply the decorator to each command that needs it. Audit candidates: `task add`, `task update`, `task remove`, `task status`, `task move`, `task claim`, `task release`, `worktree land`, plus any session-status/session-messaging commands that touch `emit_event`. Concrete list is part of the implementation PR.
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py`, `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`, `src/endless/session_status_cmd.py` — function signatures gain `no_session: bool` and thread it to the `emit_event` call.

## Verification

1. From a bare shell with no `TMUX`, no `ENDLESS_SESSION_ID`, no sibling Claude pane:
   ```sh
   endless task add "probe" --type chore --no-session
   ```
   Should succeed. The emitted event in `.endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl` has `actor.kind="system"` and no `actor.session_id`.
2. The same command WITHOUT `--no-session` still refuses, with the updated error that includes the new bullet.
3. From a Claude pane (control case), `endless task add "probe"` without `--no-session` still works as before — no regression on the attributed-default path.
4. Run the flag on each other patched verb (`task update`, `task remove`, etc.) to confirm parity.
5. Spot-check: events from a `--no-session` `task add` flow appear in the JSONL ledger; they DO NOT appear in `session_tasks` (no session to attribute the touch to) — verify by querying after a bypass-filed task.
6. Lint check: every command whose body invokes `emit_event(actor_kind="cli"|"hook", ...)` either has `@no_session_option` applied OR is documented in the PR description as deliberately omitted (with reason).


## Edge case: `--db sandbox` from a plain shell in a self-dev worktree (added 2026-06-14)

Surfaced during E-1555 verification (2026-06-12). Mike ran `endless task add ... --db sandbox` from a plain zsh shell inside the E-1555 worktree, expecting the sibling-pane resolver (layer 4 of `_current_endless_session_id`) to pick up the orchestrator Claude session in `%204`. Instead the gate fired with "Cannot determine the Endless session for this pane."

**Mechanism:** the sibling-pane lookup in `_current_endless_session_id` (`task_cmd.py:2014`) calls `_live_sessions(project_root)` (`session_cmd.py:1041`), which shells out to `endless-go --db sandbox session-query list-live`. The orchestrator Claude session's row lives in the **main** DB (`process='%204'`, `state='working'`) — it was created by `task claim` against `--db main`. The sandbox DB contains only the seeded `00000000-…` placeholder (`process=''`, no pane binding) from `seed_worktree.go:82-89`. Result: under `--db sandbox` the resolver is structurally blind to the orchestrator session row, so layers 3 and 4 fall through and the gate refuses.

```
$ uv run endless sql --db sandbox "SELECT id, session_id, process, state FROM sessions"
   1  00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000             working
$ uv run endless sql --db main    "SELECT id, session_id, process, state FROM sessions WHERE state!='ended' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 2"
 638  258da5b5-…                              %207     working
 637  c33dac5f-…                              %204     working   <- orchestrator
```

Same shape as the original E-1444 reproducer (plain shell → gate refuses), but the cause is different: not "no session exists anywhere," but "session exists in main DB, this command reads sandbox DB." Same `--no-session` escape hatch resolves it for the user, since the user just wants to write a test event without attribution. So this case is **covered by E-1444's planned fix** — but worth documenting two things:

1. **Verification step to add:** after `--no-session` lands, smoke-test a self-dev sandbox emission from a plain shell next to a live Claude pane (the case above). Confirm `--no-session` succeeds and the resulting event has `actor.kind="system"` in the sandbox JSONL ledger.
2. **Not addressed by --no-session, deliberately:** the underlying cross-DB resolver gap (sandbox-DB read can't see main-DB session rows) remains. Fixing it properly would mean either teaching `_live_sessions` to consult the main DB for session lookups even when `--db sandbox` is active, or seeding the sandbox sessions table with a row reflecting the spawning Claude session's pane. Both are larger changes than this task's scope. If `--no-session` proves sufficient in practice, the cross-DB lookup gap can stay open; if Mike finds himself wanting attributed sandbox events from a plain shell, file a follow-up.

## Related (updated 2026-06-14)

- E-1401 — introduced the attribution gate. This task softens the user-facing edge, not the gate's correctness contract.
- E-1242 — `task claim --force` precedent for "no session binding required" override, kept distinct from this new flag.
- E-1337 — worktree land + reaper task whose verification discovered the gate's user-facing pain point.
- E-1281 — self-dev DB sandbox; the cross-DB resolver gap noted above is an architectural consequence of that design.
- E-1555 — surfaced the sandbox-vs-main edge case above during verification.
- Follow-up filed (see task ledger): default of `endless task update --text` to stop auto-creating worktrees; surfaced during this task's plan attach.

fields.outcome: Same scope as E-1367 (consolidate endless-* Go binaries into single endless-go with subcommands), which landed first. cmd/ now contains only endless-go; consolidation complete.

fields.description: E-1450 (landed) routes hook-fired DB writes to the real DB unconditionally via monitor.ForceRealDB() at hook process entry — correct for production hook events fired by Claude Code, but leaves no opt-in for a developer who wants to exercise 'endless-go hook' directly against a sandbox DB to validate hook behavior without polluting the real ledger. Extend the existing --db main|sandbox flag (E-1429 / E-1476) to the 'endless-go hook' subcommand: --db sandbox skips the ForceRealDB override and lets XDG_CONFIG_HOME-resolved sandbox routing prevail; --db main matches current default; no flag keeps today's silent real-DB routing (Claude Code fires the hook without the flag). Reuses the established routing pattern — no new binary, no wrapper, no env var. Until this lands, hook tests must inject DB handles via touchSessionDB and equivalents.

payload.title: Fix project_next.go's broken project_next_items references after E-1434 schema rename
payload.description: E-1434's schema rename (project_next_items -> project_next_tasks) was applied to schema.sql + the change-file but not to the Go code that reads/writes the table. Production INSERT at internal/events/project_next.go:155 still names project_next_items; 4 test queries in internal/events/project_next_test.go (lines 95, 128, 168, 207) also still use the old name. Three TestExecProjectNextRevised_* tests fail with 'no such table: project_next_items' on any DB built from current schema.sql.

fields.text:
# E-1552 — Decide endless task spawn behavior when invoked on various task types

## 1. Scope

Decide how `endless task spawn` behaves across the four task types
(`task`, `bug`, `research`, `epic`), each combined with the state of the
task's children (zero / all `needs_plan` / all `ready` / all `in_progress`
/ all terminal / mixed). Today's spawn is type-blind and assumes a leaf
implementation task; that breaks for `epic` (a container per E-1537) and
is imprecise for `bug` and `research` whose deliverable shapes differ.

The impetus is the introduction of `epic` via E-1537/E-1538, but the
research delivers a single coherent design covering all four typed
shapes, not just epic. Tmux-window proliferation under multi-task
coordination is a load-bearing secondary motivation.

## 2. Cases to design

Cross-product of {type} × {children-state}:

- **Types**: `task`, `bug`, `research`, `epic`
- **Children states**: zero / all `needs_plan` / all `ready` / all
  `in_progress` / all terminal / mixed

24 cells. Design must be coherent across them.

## 3. Open design questions

- **Resolution mechanism**: auto-detect from children state, `--mode`
  flag, interactive choice, or combination?
- **Claim binding**: spawn pre-claim the parent (today's flow)? For
  epic, does the coordinator claim children, or do dispatched sessions
  claim themselves?
- **Worktree creation timing**: per-child worktrees at coordinator
  dispatch vs. dispatched-session self-claim?
- **Background-session integration** (Agent View / `claude --bg`): can
  spawn dispatch via bg agents to avoid tmux-window proliferation?
  What carrier mechanism binds bg agents to endless tasks?
- **Worktree conflict with Claude's `isolation:"worktree"`**: how does
  endless coexist with Claude's nested worktree behavior under bg?
- **DB schema for bg agents**: how does `sessions` model background
  agents (no tmux_pane) and the coordinator→bg-agent view-switching?
- **Attach mechanisms**: opening an existing bg agent in a new tmux
  window vs. the current pane?
- **Template format and location**: stay with Python `string.Template`,
  move to Go `text/template`, or jinja2? Where do templates live?
- **Throttling**: should endless enforce / warn / ignore parallel bg
  agent limits (quota burns at N×, community sweet spot ~3-5)?
- **Session lifecycle**: recovery after machine sleep / shutdown / tmux
  crash — what does endless need to surface?
- **Explore sub-agent dispatch** for the planning case: does endless
  own template prompts, or does the coordinator construct them?
- **Relationship to E-1136**: interactive coordinator (this work) vs.
  unattended daemon (E-1136) — co-exist or one obsoletes the other?
- **One-session-per-task invariant**: today's assumption. Does bg-agent
  dispatch + coordinator viewing require bending it?

## 4. Out of scope

- Implementation of the design (filed as a follow-up epic).
- The `WorktreeCreate` hook integration as a long-term replacement for
  `bgIsolation:"none"` (filed as maybe-task E-1559).
- Non-Claude agent harness carrier mechanism (filed as maybe-task
  E-1558).
- Session restoration commands (filed as next-phase E-1560, E-1561).

## 5. Inputs

- `docs/research-2026-06-10-claude-multi-agent-usage.md` — multi-agent
  orchestration research surfacing Model 1 (planning orchestrator) and
  Model 2 (implementation dispatcher).
- `docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md` (delivered by
  E-1557) — Claude Code Agent View mechanics: `claude --bg`,
  `claude attach`, `worktree.bgIsolation`, session lifecycle.
- E-1537 §6.5 — coordinator session semantics this work extends.
- E-1538 — `task_types` FK seeding (gates implementation, in flight).

## 6. Deliverable

Decisions on each of the §3 open questions, captured in
`tasks.outcome`. Implementation work filed as a follow-up epic with
per-implementation child tasks. Decision record via
`endless decision add` for any design choices that transcend a single
implementation task.

payload.title: Implement type-aware endless task spawn and bg-agent dispatch from E-1552 design
payload.description: Implement E-1552's design: type-aware spawn handoffs (task/bug/research/epic variants), bg-agent dispatch via claude --bg --name carrier, bgIsolation:'none' wiring, sessions schema additions (active_epic_id, kind discriminator), attach verbs (spawn --attach for new window, task attach for current pane), Go text/template renderer, soft-warning throttling, guide doc updates. Gated on E-1538 (task_types FK seeding) landing first.

payload.title: Migrate spawn handoff templates from Python string.Template to Go text/template renderer
payload.description: Add hidden `endless internal template render <name>` subcommand that reads JSON vars on stdin and writes rendered output on stdout. Convert existing docs/templates/handoff.md from $var syntax to {{.Var}} syntax. Move templates to top-level templates/ directory. Python spawn shells out per render.

payload.title: Add per-type handoff variants for task/bug/research/epic
payload.description: Split single handoff into four per-type templates at templates/handoff/{task,bug,research,epic}.md.tmpl. Renderer selects by joining tasks.type_id to task_types.slug. Each variant frames the work appropriately: task (today's flow, verify end-state), bug (reproduce-first), research (findings-as-deliverable, completed end-state), epic (coordinator role, children-state-aware). Unknown/null type falls back to task.

payload.title: Compute and inject children-state summary into epic handoff at render time
payload.description: At handoff-render time for epic spawn, query SELECT status, count(*) FROM tasks WHERE parent_id = ? GROUP BY status, format as a state breakdown ('3 ready, 2 needs_plan, 1 in_progress, 4 terminal'), pass as a template var. Coordinator picks operational mode in-session based on the breakdown. Includes the coordinator-template SET at templates/coordinator/{explore-child-context,plan-child,dispatch-child-impl,verify-child}.md.tmpl.

payload.title: Add --bg flag to endless task spawn that dispatches via claude --bg --name carrier
payload.description: Add --bg to endless task spawn. Flow: validate done-ish-status gate, pre-claim (worktree + status flip), render type-appropriate handoff to temp file, launch `claude --bg --name 'E-<task_id>' "<rendered>"` as subprocess, capture short ID from stdout (parsing the 'backgrounded · <id> · <name>' line), write binding row to sessions (task_id, claude_short_id, kind='background', active_epic_id, started_at). --name IS the carrier; no DB-token, no env-var. SessionStart hook may still fire and decorate with full session_id.

payload.title: Add worktree.bgIsolation:'none' to just claude-settings-init output
payload.description: Extend just claude-settings-init to write worktree.bgIsolation:'none' into worktree-local .claude/settings.json. Prevents Claude from creating a nested .claude/worktrees/ under endless's tracker worktree when bg agents launch. Required before bg-agent dispatch is usable. v2.1.143+ feature.

payload.title: Add endless task spawn --attach and endless task attach for bg-agent viewing
payload.description: Two attach verbs. (1) endless task spawn --attach <id>: opens NEW tmux window via tmux new-window + send-keys 'claude attach <short-id>'. Used when a bg agent needs dedicated focus separate from the coordinator's pane. (2) endless task attach <id>: execs current pane via os.execvp('claude', ['attach', short_id]). Used from a fresh shell to drop into a running bg agent. Both leverage claude attach (Claude's bg→fg view mechanism). Detaching leaves the bg agent running.

payload.title: Add sessions.active_epic_id column and kind discriminator for bg-agent rows
payload.description: Schema additions: (a) sessions.active_epic_id INTEGER REFERENCES tasks(id) NULL. Coordinator row: stable=epic id, active_task_id changes as user switches view. Bg agent row: active_task_id=child task id, active_epic_id=parent epic id. Non-epic foreground: active_epic_id=NULL. (b) sessions.kind discriminator: 'tmux' or 'background'. tmux_pane becomes nullable for 'background' rows. Window-name renderer reads active_epic_id + active_task_id: NOT NULL AND != → [E-EEEE:E-CCCC]; NOT NULL AND = → [E-EEEE]; NULL → [E-NNNN]. Focused-bg-agent status is derived from the coordinator row, not stored.

payload.title: Add soft warning at endless task spawn --bg when N bg agents already active
payload.description: Configurable threshold in .endless/config.json as bg_throttle_warn (default 3). At dispatch, count active bg agents for the project; if >= threshold, emit a soft warning explaining the quota cost (N× linear) and the community-observed thread-limit risk. Does NOT block dispatch — coordinator decides. Source: docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md §7.

payload.title: Document spawn/coordinator/bg-dispatch patterns in endless guide orchestration section
payload.description: Update endless guide orchestration section to cover: per-type spawn behavior (4 types), bg-agent dispatch via task spawn --bg, the two attach verbs, coordinator pattern for epic spawn, children-state-aware operational modes, soft throttling warning, session lifecycle (sleep/shutdown/tmux-crash recovery pointing at E-1560/E-1561). Replaces today's tmux-only spawn write-up.

payload.title: Document research-task model (text=request, outcome=results) in endless guide
payload.description: Update endless guide to surface the research-type task model decided in E-1552's discussion: tasks.text holds the research request (scope, open questions, inputs); tasks.outcome holds the deliverable (findings, decisions, pointers to implementation work). Large standalone deliverables (research reports, decision documents) live alongside the task. References the per-task-directory restructure (filed separately) and E-1531 typed-content evolution.

fields.outcome:
# Decisions

## CLI surface
Spawn's CLI flow stays uniform across all 24 type×children-state cells; the handoff template branches by type. Same pattern endless already uses for type-conditional behavior (verb gates, completable-status gates).

## Per-type spawn behavior
| Type | Spawn behavior | Handoff variant | End-state |
|---|---|---|---|
| `task` | Today's fg tmux + claim | `handoff/task.md.tmpl` | `verify` → confirmed |
| `bug` | Same as task; reproduce-first framing | `handoff/bug.md.tmpl` | `verify` → confirmed |
| `research` | Same as task; findings-as-deliverable | `handoff/research.md.tmpl` | `completed` (verb gate per ED-1502) |
| `epic` | Fg tmux coordinator + children-state summary | `handoff/epic.md.tmpl` | `completed` via auto-derivation (E-1537 §4) |

All handoffs include the child count and recommend `--children` to the spawned session.

## Bg-agent dispatch (epic children)
`endless task spawn --bg <id>` dispatches via `claude --bg --name "E-<task_id>"`. Spawn captures the short ID from stdout, writes the binding row directly to `sessions`. No DB-token mechanism, no env-var carrier — `--name` IS the carrier. SessionStart hook still fires inside the bg agent (source=`startup`); decorative for binding, not load-bearing.

## bgIsolation requirement
`worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` REQUIRED for v1. Added to `just claude-settings-init` output. Prevents Claude from creating a nested worktree under `.claude/worktrees/` that would defeat endless's pre-claim. Long-term alternative (`WorktreeCreate` hook) deferred until Agent View exits research preview, given current open-issue density (~11 open issues, two June 2026 regressions including the critical #68214 isolation-failure bug).

## DB schema additions
- `sessions.active_epic_id` (nullable FK to tasks).
- `sessions.kind` discriminator: `tmux` or `background`. `tmux_pane` nullable for background.
- Window-name rendering: `active_epic_id NOT NULL AND != active_task_id` → `[E-EEEE:E-CCCC]`; `NOT NULL AND =` → `[E-EEEE]`; `NULL` → `[E-NNNN]`.
- "Focused bg agent" is derived from the coordinator row, not stored.

## Attach verbs
- `endless task spawn --attach <id>` — new tmux window via `tmux new-window` + send-keys `claude attach <short-id>`. Used when a bg agent needs dedicated focus.
- `endless task attach <id>` — exec current pane via `os.execvp("claude", ["attach", short_id])`. Used from a fresh shell.

Both leverage `claude attach` (Claude's bg→fg view mechanism per docs/research-2026-06-12-claude-background-agents.md §3). Detach paths leave the bg agent running; only `claude stop` or `Ctrl+X` ends it.

## Templates
Migration from Python `string.Template` to **Go `text/template`**. Renderer: hidden `endless internal template render <name>` subcommand reading JSON vars on stdin. Location: `templates/` top-level. Per-type at `templates/handoff/{task,bug,research,epic}.md.tmpl`. Coordinator templates SET at `templates/coordinator/{explore-child-context,plan-child,dispatch-child-impl,verify-child}.md.tmpl`. Endless owns the template SET; coordinators can override locally but default to template.

## Throttling
Soft warning at dispatch when N bg agents already active for the project (default threshold 3, configurable as `bg_throttle_warn` in `.endless/config.json`). Does NOT block dispatch — coordinator decides. Rationale: bg quota burns linearly with parallel count (10× for 10), community sweet spot 3–5.

## Children-state computation (epic handoff)
At render time: `SELECT status, count(*) FROM tasks WHERE parent_id = ? GROUP BY status`. Coordinator picks operational mode in-session from the breakdown:
- **Zero**: drive decomposition (plan-mode + AskUserQuestion + parallel Explore sub-agents).
- **All `needs_plan`**: Model 1 — planning orchestrator drafts plans inline.
- **All `ready`**: Model 2 — dispatcher fans out via `endless task spawn --bg <child-id>`.
- **All `in_progress`**: observe, no dispatch.
- **All terminal**: ask whether to reopen.
- **Mixed**: surface breakdown, ask user.

No CLI `--mode` flag; coordinator decides in-session.

## Session lifecycle
Bg agents survive: fg Claude exit, terminal close, tmux pane close, machine sleep (v2.1.142+). Die on: machine shutdown, 1h idle (unpinned), `claude stop`. Recovery commands filed as E-1560 (reboot via `claude respawn`) and E-1561 (tmux crash via reconnect).

## Relationship to E-1136
Co-exist. E-1552 = interactive coordinator. E-1136 = unattended daemon. Different operational shapes, not alternatives.

## One-session-per-task invariant
Stays as today's assumption. Open to revisiting when a concrete use case bends it.

# Implementation

**Follow-up epic:** **E-1564** — Implement type-aware endless task spawn and bg-agent dispatch from E-1552 design.
Blocked-by E-1538 (task_types FK seeding).

**Children of E-1564:**
- E-1565 — Migrate spawn handoff templates from Python string.Template to Go text/template renderer
- E-1566 — Add per-type handoff variants for task/bug/research/epic
- E-1567 — Compute and inject children-state summary into epic handoff at render time
- E-1568 — Add --bg flag to endless task spawn that dispatches via claude --bg --name carrier
- E-1569 — Add worktree.bgIsolation:'none' to just claude-settings-init output
- E-1570 — Add endless task spawn --attach and endless task attach for bg-agent viewing
- E-1571 — Add sessions.active_epic_id column and kind discriminator for bg-agent rows
- E-1572 — Add soft warning at endless task spawn --bg when N bg agents already active
- E-1573 — Document spawn/coordinator/bg-dispatch patterns in endless guide orchestration section

# Related tasks filed during this work

**Maybe-phase** (long-term direction, no commitment):
- E-1558 — Extend endless sessions table to support non-Claude agent harnesses
- E-1559 — Implement WorktreeCreate hook integration to bridge Claude worktrees to endless

**Next-phase** (real follow-ups, not blocking):
- E-1560 — Add endless session restore to recover bg agents after machine shutdown/reboot
- E-1561 — Add endless tmux restore to reconnect to bg agents and respawn coordinator after tmux crash
- E-1574 — Document research-task model (text=request, outcome=results) in endless guide

# Decision recorded

ED- — Research-type tasks: text holds the request, outcome holds the results. (Recorded via `endless decision add`; links --about E-1552.)

payload.title: Research-type tasks: text holds the request, outcome holds the results
payload.description: Research-type tasks split input/output across two existing fields rather than overloading one. tasks.text holds the research REQUEST (scope, open questions, inputs); tasks.outcome holds the DELIVERABLE (findings, decisions, pointers to follow-up implementation). Today tasks.text mirrors to .endless/plans/E-NNN.md; tasks.outcome lives in the DB only. A future per-task-directory restructure (filed separately) will mirror both fields to disk; after E-1531 lands typed task_content rows, the overload moves into explicit content types (research_request, research_outcome, etc.). Surfaced during E-1552 when 'research-task text holding both the input questions and the output design plan' produced a contradiction.
